subject: Public Health
title: Mental Health in Conflict Zones
description: A quick, practical guide to Mental Health in Conflict Zones.

hook.text: In conflict-affected areas, one in five people has a mental health condition—double the global average.

buildup.text: War, displacement, and loss of family create mass trauma that overwhelms any health system.

discovery.text: Yet mental health receives less than 1% of humanitarian aid—even in the worst emergencies.

twist.text: Low-cost interventions—group therapy led by trained laypeople—work even in active conflict zones.

climax.text: The WHO's mhGAP program trains non-specialists to deliver mental health care where no psychiatrists exist.

punchline.text: War destroys minds as well as buildings. Both need rebuilding.

quiz.question: What fraction of humanitarian aid goes to mental health?
